title: hedera helix subsp. canariensis
intro: Hedera helix subsp. canariensis, also known as the Madeira or Canary Island ivy, is a subspecies of the common ivy plant native to the Canary Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress. The health benefits of Hedera helix subsp. canariensis include reducing anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
